metadata.title: Gulf Infrastructure Damage Database
metadata.scope: 40+ energy assets across 9 countries, plus data centers and military infrastructure

assets.name: South Pars / Asaluyeh Gas Complex
assets.prewar_capacity: Largest natural gas reservoir globally; supplies ~70% of Iran domestic gas needs; feeds Asaluyeh onshore complex with 24+ processing phases
assets.details: Israeli strikes damaged 4 gas-treatment plants at Asaluyeh — dehydration and sweetening units destroyed. Fires raged for days, forcing emergency flaring wasting millions of cubic meters. Critical H2S removal and water-separation infrastructure lost.
assets.current_status: Partially offline. 4 treatment plants destroyed. Emergency flaring ongoing. Domestic gas supply severely impacted.

assets.name: Kharg Island Oil Export Terminal
assets.prewar_capacity: 1.6 million b/d crude and condensates export capacity; Iran's primary oil-export terminal
assets.details: Multiple missile strikes cratered storage tanks, severed loading jetties, ignited fires consuming estimated 300,000 barrels. Satellite imagery revealed widespread collateral damage to crude-storage infrastructure and associated pipelines.
assets.current_status: Operational at reduced capacity for ghost fleet loading. Trump threatened further strikes by April 6 deadline. ~1.5M bpd loading continuing (mostly ghost fleet/Chinese circuit).

assets.name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
assets.prewar_capacity: 1,000 MW nuclear reactor; Iran's sole nuclear power plant
assets.details: Not directly targeted but sits uncomfortably close to damaged gas infrastructure that supplies cooling systems. Fears of secondary power disruptions if gas supply to backup cooling fails.
assets.current_status: Not directly hit. Cooling infrastructure concerns remain if gas supply deteriorates further.

assets.name: Isfahan Gas Pressure Regulation Station
assets.prewar_capacity: Major gas-pressure regulation station feeding power plants and petrochemical facilities across central Iran
assets.details: Gas-pressure regulation station hit, disrupting flows to power plants and petrochemical facilities in Isfahan province.
assets.current_status: Offline. Disruption to power plants and petrochemical facilities.

assets.name: Iran National Oil Export Infrastructure
assets.prewar_capacity: 1.69 million b/d crude exports pre-war
assets.details: Pre-war exports of 1.69M b/d fallen to ~500K b/d — two-thirds reduction. Nationwide blackouts reported. Fuel shortages grip major cities. ~48M barrels sit in floating storage offshore on shadow fleet tankers. Producing at ~30% of pre-war export capacity.
assets.current_status: ~30% export capacity. 1.5M bpd crude loadings via ghost fleet/toll circuit. Domestic supply in crisis — nationwide blackouts, fuel shortages.

assets.name: Ras Laffan Industrial City
assets.prewar_capacity: 14 operational LNG trains, 77 million tonnes/year LNG capacity; world's largest concentration of LNG export trains
assets.details: Iranian missiles struck March 18-19, igniting fires across at least 6 of 14 trains. QatarEnergy declared force majeure on all contracts within hours. 17% of nameplate LNG capacity offline. 3-5 year repair timeline confirmed by QatarEnergy CEO.
assets.current_status: Still offline. 3-5 year repair. Cryogenic components from Japan/Germany/US foundries at capacity. Fires may still be partially active.

assets.name: Pearl Gas-to-Liquids Plant
assets.prewar_capacity: World's largest GTL facility (Shell JV). Converts natural gas into ultra-clean diesel, kerosene, lubricants via Fischer-Tropsch technology. 140,000 b/d capacity.
assets.details: Direct hits to synthesis reactors and hydrocrackers. Catalyst beds and high-pressure vessels require custom fabrication and certification under international safety standards. Extraordinarily difficult to repair quickly.
assets.current_status: Offline. Repair timeline longer than LNG — Fischer-Tropsch reactors require custom fabrication. Catalyst beds and high-pressure vessels must be custom-made.

assets.name: North Field LNG Expansion
assets.prewar_capacity: 6 additional LNG trains scheduled for completion by 2027; would have added ~48 mtpa capacity
assets.details: Suspended indefinitely. Repair crews cannot begin full assessments until fires extinguished and unexploded ordnance cleared. Future expansion plans frozen.
assets.current_status: Suspended indefinitely. Construction halted. No timeline for resumption.

assets.name: Ras Tanura Refinery and Export Complex
assets.prewar_capacity: 550,000 b/d processing capacity; primary loading terminal for Arab Light crude
assets.details: Early drone strikes damaged distillation units, tank farms, and control rooms. Facility shut for days before partial restart at reduced rates.
assets.current_status: Partial restart at reduced rates. Distillation units, tank farms, control rooms damaged.

assets.name: SAMREF Refinery (Yanbu)
assets.prewar_capacity: Major joint venture with ExxonMobil at Yanbu on the Red Sea
assets.details: Direct missile impacts destroyed catalytic cracker and associated storage. ExxonMobil JV facility severely impacted.
assets.current_status: Catalytic cracker destroyed. Offline for major repairs — 18-24 month timeline.

assets.name: Shaybah Oil Field
assets.prewar_capacity: 1 million b/d extra-light crude from carbonate reservoirs in the Empty Quarter
assets.details: Repeatedly targeted. Wellheads damaged, gathering lines severed, associated gas-processing plants forced offline.
assets.current_status: Wellheads and gathering lines damaged. Gas processing offline. Production severely reduced.

assets.name: Yanbu Export Terminal
assets.prewar_capacity: Major Red Sea export port for Saudi crude and refined products
assets.details: Export port suspended loadings for more than a week.
assets.current_status: Suspended for 1+ week. Partially resumed operations.

assets.name: Jubail Petrochemical Complex
assets.prewar_capacity: World-scale ethylene crackers and fertilizer plants; major petrochemical hub
assets.details: Collateral damage from debris and power-grid failures. Ethylene crackers and fertilizer plants impacted.
assets.current_status: Collateral damage from debris. Power grid failures impacting operations.

assets.name: Saudi Aramco Master Gas System
assets.prewar_capacity: Collects associated gas from multiple fields to feed domestic power and industry; integrated pipeline network
assets.details: Operating at 60% capacity. Associated gas collection from multiple fields disrupted.
assets.current_status: Operating at 60% capacity. Feed to domestic power and industry constrained.

assets.name: Ruwais Refinery Complex
assets.prewar_capacity: 837,000 b/d; one of the largest refineries in the world (ADNOC)
assets.details: Shut after drone-induced fire gutted hydrotreating units. ADNOC complex severely impacted.
assets.current_status: Shut down. Hydrotreating units gutted by fire. 18-24 month repair timeline.

assets.name: Habshan Gas Processing Hub
assets.prewar_capacity: Central gas-processing hub — sweetens and fractionates associated gas from Bab and Bu Hasa fields; 4 processing trains
assets.details: Struck by debris. 3 of 4 gas processing trains remain offline.
assets.current_status: 3 of 4 trains offline. Partial operation only.

assets.name: Fujairah Oil Terminal and Port
assets.prewar_capacity: Hormuz bypass terminal on Gulf of Oman; designed to export oil without transiting Strait; major bunkering hub
assets.details: Endured repeated closures due to missile threats and shipping insurance spikes. Bypass designed for temporary Hormuz disruption — not sustained closure under fire.
assets.current_status: Repeated closures from missile threats and insurance spikes. Limited protection against sustained Hormuz closure.

assets.name: Jebel Ali Port
assets.prewar_capacity: Region's largest container port and logistics hub; 19.3M TEU capacity
assets.details: Suspended operations for days, snarling supply chains for spare parts and chemicals needed elsewhere in the Gulf.
assets.current_status: Operations resumed but supply chains for spare parts and chemicals disrupted region-wide.

assets.name: Das Island LNG Plant
assets.prewar_capacity: LNG exports from associated gas from offshore fields
assets.details: Running at minimal capacity because outbound tankers cannot safely navigate the Strait of Hormuz.
assets.current_status: Minimal capacity. Tanker transit unsafe through Strait.

assets.name: Shah Gas Field
assets.prewar_capacity: Sour-gas development critical for UAE domestic supply; ADNOC operated
assets.details: Hit directly, forcing emergency imports from neighbors already struggling with their own losses.
assets.current_status: Direct hit. Emergency imports from strained neighbors.

assets.name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery
assets.prewar_capacity: Part of Kuwait's 700,000+ b/d refining complex (with Mina Abdullah). Atmospheric distillation columns and catalytic reformers.
assets.details: Struck early. Fires destroyed atmospheric distillation columns and catalytic reformers. Re-hit on April 3, 2026 — damage compounding. ~50% of Kuwait's oil output remains offline.
assets.current_status: Re-hit April 3. Damage compounding. Atmospheric columns and reformers destroyed. ~50% of Kuwait output offline.

assets.name: Mina Abdullah Refinery
assets.prewar_capacity: Part of Kuwait's 700,000+ b/d refining complex (with Mina Al-Ahmadi)
assets.details: Struck early alongside Mina Al-Ahmadi. Atmospheric distillation columns and catalytic reformers damaged.
assets.current_status: Damaged. ~50% of Kuwait output offline.

assets.name: Bapco Energies Refinery (Sitra)
assets.prewar_capacity: 400,000 b/d processing capacity
assets.details: Declared force majeure after precision strikes on its visbreaker and storage tanks.
assets.current_status: Force majeure declared. Visbreaker and storage tanks damaged.

assets.name: Sohar Export Terminal
assets.prewar_capacity: Major Omani export terminal for crude and refined products
assets.details: Closed temporarily after threats and minor damage to loading arms.
assets.current_status: Temporarily closed. Minor loading arm damage.

assets.name: Mina Al Fahal Export Terminal
assets.prewar_capacity: Oman's primary crude oil export terminal near Muscat
assets.details: Closed temporarily after threats and minor damage.
assets.current_status: Temporarily closed.

assets.name: Salalah Export Terminal
assets.prewar_capacity: Southern Oman export terminal; LNG and container operations
assets.details: Closed temporarily after threats.
assets.current_status: Temporarily closed.

assets.name: Lanaz Refinery (Erbil)
assets.prewar_capacity: Regional refinery near Erbil in Kurdistan
assets.details: Targeted during initial strikes. Damage details limited.
assets.current_status: Damaged. Limited details on current status.

assets.name: Majnoon Oil Field
assets.prewar_capacity: Major southern Iraq oil field; ~400,000 b/d capacity
assets.details: Targeted in southern Iraq. Wellhead and infrastructure damage.
assets.current_status: Production down. Southern Iraqi fields down ~30% overall.

assets.name: Basra Export Terminals
assets.prewar_capacity: 3.63 million b/d export capacity; Iraq's primary southern export infrastructure
assets.details: Terminals are undamaged but sea lanes are blocked by Strait closure. 3.63M b/d produced but cannot be shipped. Iraq beginning to truck oil via Syria as bypass.
assets.current_status: Terminals undamaged. 3.63M b/d trapped — sea lanes blocked. Iraq rerouting via Syria land bridge.

assets.name: Iraq-Turkey and Intra-Gulf Pipelines
assets.prewar_capacity: Major pipeline network including Iraq-Turkey line (1.6M b/d) and intra-Gulf feeders
assets.details: Severed in multiple places, creating bottlenecks that compound terminal damage. Cross-regional flows disrupted.
assets.current_status: Severed in multiple locations. Bottlenecks compounding.

assets.name: Amazon Data Center (UAE)
assets.prewar_capacity: AWS cloud infrastructure serving Middle East region; hyperscale facility
assets.details: Early March drones struck Amazon data centers in UAE. US tech infrastructure confirmed as kinetic target by IRGC.
assets.current_status: Damaged by early March drone strikes. Compute thesis confirmed at kinetic level.

assets.name: Amazon Data Center (Bahrain)
assets.prewar_capacity: AWS cloud infrastructure serving Middle East region; hyperscale facility
assets.details: Early March drones struck Amazon data centers in Bahrain alongside UAE strikes.
assets.current_status: Damaged by early March drone strikes.

assets.name: Oracle Data Center (Dubai)
assets.prewar_capacity: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure serving Middle East enterprise customers
assets.details: IRGC struck Oracle's Dubai data center. Confirmed hit following earlier Amazon/Bahrain strikes. US tech infrastructure now actively being targeted in Gulf.
assets.current_status: Struck April 3. Compute thesis no longer theoretical — data centers are kinetic targets.

assets.name: Kuwait Fuel Depots
assets.prewar_capacity: National fuel storage and distribution depots
assets.details: Iran retaliatory strikes hit Kuwait fuel depots on April 3, 2026 following Trump address.
assets.current_status: Hit April 3. Fuel depots damaged in retaliatory barrage.

assets.name: Kuwait International Airport Fuel Tanks
assets.prewar_capacity: Aviation fuel storage and supply for Kuwait International Airport
assets.details: Iran drone struck fuel tanks at Kuwait International Airport. New target type — aviation fuel infrastructure.
assets.current_status: Fuel tanks struck by drone April 1. Aviation supply disrupted.

assets.name: Bubiyan Island US Military Depot
assets.prewar_capacity: US military logistics depot on Bubiyan Island, Kuwait — staging and supply facility
assets.details: Strike on US military depot on Bubiyan Island, Kuwait. Part of Iran's continued strikes on US military infrastructure across Gulf. Collateral context in US 48-hour ceasefire proposal rejection.
assets.current_status: Struck ~Apr 2-4. US military logistics infrastructure damaged.

assets.name: US Military Bases Across Gulf (7+ bases)
assets.prewar_capacity: US military infrastructure across Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE — radar, satellite comms, aircraft
assets.details: Iran struck radar, satellite comms, and mission-critical aircraft at 7+ US bases across Gulf since Feb 28. E-3 Sentry AWACS damaged at Prince Sultan AB (Mar 27). Five KC-135 tankers damaged Mar 13. AN/TPY-2 THAAD radar destroyed in Jordan. 66-75% of available E-3 fleet already deployed — losses tightening coverage gaps.
assets.current_status: Extensive damage across 7+ bases. E-3 AWACS damaged, KC-135 tankers hit, THAAD radar destroyed.

assets.name: Prince Sultan Air Base
assets.prewar_capacity: Major US Air Force base in Saudi Arabia; host to E-3 Sentry AWACS and fighter operations
assets.details: E-3 Sentry AWACS aircraft damaged at Prince Sultan AB on March 27, 2026. 66-75% of available E-3 fleet already deployed — losses tightening coverage gaps.
assets.current_status: E-3 AWACS damaged. Operational capability reduced.

assets.name: Shuaiba Refinery
assets.prewar_capacity: Southern Kuwait refinery; part of national refining complex
assets.details: Part of Kuwait refining complex impacted alongside Mina Al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah strikes.
assets.current_status: Damaged. Kuwait refining capacity at ~50% output.

assets.name: ADNOC Headquarters and Operations (Abu Dhabi)
assets.prewar_capacity: Abu Dhabi National Oil Company — upstream, midstream, downstream operations; 3+ million b/d production capacity
assets.details: ADNOC's operations impacted across multiple facilities (Ruwais, Habshan, Shah). UAE faced 2,000+ aerial threats since Feb 28.
assets.current_status: Multiple facilities impacted. 2,000+ aerial threats since Feb 28.

assets.name: Strait of Hormuz
assets.prewar_capacity: ~21 million b/d crude + equivalent LNG; ~20% of global petroleum liquids transit
assets.details: Effectively closed. Mines, anti-ship missiles, IRGC fast boats. Insurance premiums up 20x to +300%. 181 vessels in March total (vs ~138/day pre-crisis). 94% YoY decline. IRGC formal toll system at Larak Island: $0.50-$2M/bbl in yuan/stablecoins. First Western ships transited Apr 4 (CMA CGM Kribi, Mitsui vessel). 2,000 vessels stranded. Iran-Oman dual-permit protocol in final stages.
assets.current_status: Closed to most traffic. IRGC toll system operational — processing ~7 transits/day (vs 138/day pre-crisis). Yuan/stablecoin toll system. First Western ships Apr 4.

iran.current_export: ~500K b/d formal + ~1.5M b/d ghost fleet loading
iran.domestic_impact: Nationwide blackouts, fuel shortages in major cities

saudi_arabia.current_production: Reduced — Ras Tanura partial restart, Shaybah damaged, master gas at 60%
saudi_arabia.spare_capacity: 4+ million b/d trapped behind Hormuz — unusable
saudi_arabia.export_constraint: Can produce but cannot ship through Hormuz

uae.current_production: Ruwais shut, Habshan at 25% capacity, Das Island minimal
uae.bypass_status: Fujairah closed repeatedly — Hormuz bypass ineffective

kuwait.new_damage: Airport fuel tanks struck Apr 1, fuel depots hit Apr 3, Bubiyan Island depot struck Apr 2

bahrain.current_status: Force majeure declared. Visbreaker and storage tanks damaged.

oman.prewar_export: Multiple terminals (Sohar, Mina Al Fahal, Salalah)
oman.current_status: Terminals temporarily closed. Attempting southern Hormuz route — intercepted by IRGC.

iraq.current_production: Southern fields down 30%. Basra 3.63M b/d trapped — terminals undamaged but sea lanes blocked.
iraq.rerouting: Trucking oil via Syria land bridge — first confirmed bypass

hormuz_traffic.event: Strait effectively closed to commercial shipping
26 vessel transits since Mar 13 via IRGC-controlled corridor (Larak Island) — zero normal route since Mar 15
First verified yuan-for-Hormuz transaction (Newvoyager). CIPS cleared. Kunlun intermediaries.
Iran operating formal yuan toll system — $0.50-1.20/bbl via Kunlun Bank, 72-96hr advance booking. ~8-10M bbl/day in yuan circuit.
Lloyd's List: 181 vessels in March total (vs ~138/day pre-crisis). 70% Iranian-linked. Clarksons: 95% down.
Bloomberg: weekly transits ticking up to 7 from 5 — selective fleet of China/Iran/non-hostile Asian partners only.
Japan paid $2M toll for two vessels. Even US allies paying IRGC tolls.
Iran crude loadings continuing at 1.5M bpd. ~1M bpd transiting strait since Mar 1. 67% of March transits had direct Iranian affiliation.

IRGC toll system accepting cryptocurrency (stablecoins) alongside yuan. VLCCs quoted up to $2M/passage. Parliament advancing toll legislation. ~2,000 vessels stranded (IMO). Hormuz traffic down 94% YoY.
Iraq begins trucking oil across Syria to bypass Hormuz. First confirmed rerouting by major Gulf exporter.
Three Omani tankers attempt southern route hugging Oman coastline — intercepted by IRGC near Musandam Peninsula. All stopped transmitting.
First Western commercial ships transit Hormuz under IRGC toll — CMA CGM Kribi and Mitsui-operated vessel.
Pakistan secured passage for 20 vessels through toll system. IRGC intercepting alternative Omani routes.
Insurance at +300%. UANI: 27+ ghost fleet tankers laden with Iranian oil in Gulf, ~38M bbl loaded since war start, ~$3B+ revenue for IRGC.

price_divergence.brent_note: Crashed ~4.5% from $118 last week. Monthly gain +27-40%.
price_divergence.gold_note: Countries SELLING gold reserves to raise dollars for oil purchases.
Broke above 100-day SMA at $4,617. De-escalation hopes driving.

price_divergence.brent_note: WTI spiked 11.5% to $111+. WTI briefly ABOVE Brent — rare, signals US physical supply panic.
Spot Brent physical cargo — highest since 2008. Futures $107-109 mask real tightness. $32 gap.
price_divergence.gold_note: UBS revised forecast down to $5,000 avg. Silver -3% to $72.66.

price_divergence.brent_note: Markets closed Good Friday. Spot Brent physical at $141.36. Monday gap risk significant.
price_divergence.gold_note: Markets closed. NFP ghost move implications for Monday.

repair_timelines.pearl_gtl: Longer than LNG — custom Fischer-Tropsch reactor fabrication
repair_timelines.oies_projection: Gradual Hormuz reopening to 95% by Q4 2026 (optimistic)

historical_parallels.2019_abqaiq: 4 months — fraction of current damage, no structural fires
